name: M.S. in Transportation

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: Only three courses are referenced and all are research vehicles (TRAN 700B Master's Project, TRAN 701B Master's Thesis, TRAN 792 Pre-Doctoral Research); no transportation coursework or plan of study is captured, so progression cannot be assessed. A transportation M.S. should surface traffic engineering, planning, ITS, and safety courses that are entirely absent here.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the full plan of study with a transportation core (traffic operations, planning, ITS, safety) leading into the project/thesis so a coherent trajectory is visible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and missingCourseCount is 0 only because the three referenced items are thesis/project/pre-doctoral credits with essentially no prerequisites; the graph is empty rather than healthy and shows no sequencing.
dimensions.suggestion: List the graduate transportation coursework and its prerequisites so a real prerequisite structure (e.g., a statistics/traffic-flow foundation gating advanced ITS courses) appears.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, and with only research-credit courses shown the page gives prospective students nothing to judge intended competencies against.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 learning outcomes describing the transportation-planning, traffic-operations, and analytics capabilities the M.S. develops.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is a clear 30, which is transparent, but with no plan parsed (requirementGroups=0) the split between coursework and the thesis/project is not shown and creditReconcileDelta is null.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the 30-credit breakdown (required core, electives, and thesis/project options) so the balance within the stated total is visible.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The entry shows only Master's Project, Master's Thesis, and Pre-Doctoral Research; there is no overview, coursework list, plan of study, or track structure, so the page cannot orient a prospective applicant.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the catalog page with a complete transportation course list, plan of study, and thesis/non-thesis options.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: With only research-credit courses visible, the currency of the degree's actual coursework cannot be assessed; modern transportation strands (ITS, connected/automated vehicles, mobility analytics) are neither confirmed nor visible in the data.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the transportation coursework (ITS, traffic operations, planning, safety) so the degree's currency and specializations are demonstrable.

descriptionQuality.justification: The three referenced courses (TRAN 700B, TRAN 701B, TRAN 792) carry clear descriptions of the project/thesis/pre-doctoral expectations, but with only research credits present there is no basis to judge description quality across the degree's actual coursework.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are limited to advisor/program-director approval on the research credits and no coursework is present, so prerequisite clarity cannot be meaningfully assessed; the entries are unambiguous only because they are trivially few.

missingTopics.rationale: The page shows only thesis/project/pre-doctoral credits; the coursework spine (traffic engineering, planning, ITS, safety, freight/logistics) is missing from the catalog data.
No traffic-operations or flow-theory course is referenced to prepare students for a transportation thesis.
Data-driven safety analysis is a core transportation competency not represented among the three courses.
Transit and multimodal mobility planning, central to the field, are absent from the visible content.

emergingTrends.rationale: CAV technology is reshaping transportation research and practice; the degree should build and display a CAV/ITS strand.
New mobility business models are a fast-growing planning frontier a transportation M.S. should position for.
Machine learning for demand, traffic prediction, and safety is a standard research instrument for transportation students.
EV infrastructure and decarbonization of transport are emerging priorities a transportation research degree should reflect.

topSuggestions: Rebuild the catalog entry with the complete transportation coursework, since three research-credit courses cannot represent an M.S. degree.
Publish the transportation core (traffic operations, planning, ITS, safety) and a required methods/statistics foundation.
Publish the 30-credit breakdown of core, electives, and thesis/project so the balance within the stated total is visible.
Publish program learning outcomes describing transportation-planning, operations, and analytics competencies.
Surface modern electives (connected/automated vehicles, mobility analytics, electrification) so the degree's currency is demonstrable.
